mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending confidence, and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth (INDPRO) indicates robust corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved profit expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high INDPRO periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong aggregate demand, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through sustained corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: Either GDPC1 or INDPRO shows negative growth in 60-day windows, or interaction effect fails to produce significant positive returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 2%) increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or FEDFUNDS declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) and residential sales (RSAFS) indicate elevated consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved sectoral performance.
failure_condition: Either HOUST or RSAFS shows negative growth in 20-day windows, or interaction effect fails to produce significant positive returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) prompts central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or CPIAUCSL declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: A tight labor market (UNRATE < 5%) drives wage growth and corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or UNRATE rises without corresponding equity losses.
